data_out.description: OpenAPI 3.0 specification document (JSON or YAML) describing joblauncher HTTP API contract

domain_knowledge: OpenAPI 3.0 specification format and schema validation conventions for HTTP API documentation.
RESTful API design patterns: URL path structure, HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE), and request/response payload serialization in Python web frameworks.
joblauncher is a component within the MAGMa metabolomics pipeline responsible for submitting and managing computational jobs via HTTP endpoints.
Python introspection techniques: examining function signatures, type hints, decorators, and route definitions to reverse-engineer API contracts from source code.

direct_checks: file_exists: verify that joblauncher source files are present in github:NLeSC/MAGMa repository under a joblauncher/ or equivalent subdirectory
file_format_is: verify that produced artifact is valid JSON or YAML (OpenAPI 3.0 or 3.1 specification)
contains_substring: verify that output schema contains at least one 'paths' or 'endpoints' key documenting HTTP routes
contains_substring: verify that output schema declares HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH) for joblauncher endpoints
contains_substring: verify that output schema includes 'components' or 'definitions' section with request/response model schemas
script_runs: verify that produced OpenAPI artifact passes validation against openapi-generator or swagger-cli validate command without fatal errors
expert_review: Verify that extracted endpoint signatures (paths, parameters, request bodies, response codes) accurately reflect joblauncher source code; require spot-check of 3+ endpoints against raw implementation
Assess completeness of parameter documentation (required vs optional, data types, constraints) relative to actual function signatures in joblauncher source
Evaluate semantic accuracy of request and response schema definitions against actual data structures used in joblauncher handlers

Parse HTTP route definitions and endpoint handlers to extract paths, methods, and parameter names.

task_description: Inspect the MAGMa joblauncher component source files in the NLeSC/MAGMa repository and reconstruct its HTTP API contract by extracting endpoint definitions, request/response schemas, and parameters. Produce a machine-readable OpenAPI 3.0 specification document.
task_objective: Inspect the joblauncher source code in github:NLeSC/MAGMa and produce an OpenAPI 3.0 or JSON schema artifact documenting all exposed HTTP endpoints, methods, request parameters, response structures, and authentication requirements.
title: Implement the joblauncher webservice component interface for MAGMa job submission
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
workflow_description: 1. Clone the NLeSC/MAGMa repository from GitHub. 2. Locate and examine joblauncher source files (Python, configuration, and route definitions) to identify all HTTP endpoints, HTTP methods, URL patterns, and parameter names. 3. Extract request and response payload structures by analyzing function signatures, type hints, and serialization logic in the joblauncher codebase. 4. Map endpoint paths, methods, parameters (query, path, body), and response schemas into OpenAPI 3.0 format. 5. Validate the generated OpenAPI specification for structural correctness and completeness against the observed source code.
